properties.remark: REPORT FROM BOWSTRING LAKE AREA OF A LARGE AMOUNT OF HAILSTONES RANGING FROM PEA SIZE TO QUARTER SIZE. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.
Hail for up to 30 minutes. Mostly around a half inch in size, but a few were up to the size of quarters. Time estimated from radar.

Photos were received of damage to oak, pine, and popple trees with limbs ranging from 4 to 16 inches. Some of the trees had been damaged by heavy snow this past winter and some had broken at rotted out portions, but the majority were in good health. Report was delayed as it was a vacation property. Time estimated by radar.
Report of about 5 large trees down due to damaging winds. Approximately a foot to foot and a half in diameter. Some trees uprooted, others snapped in half. Some appeared healthy, some not.
Report of a power pole down that caught fire due to strong thunderstorm winds between Bayfield and Red Cliff. Time estimated by radar.
Corrects previous tstm wnd dmg report from Ashland. 8-inch tree limb down. Time estimated by radar. Corrected time of damage.
Tree of unknown diameter and health reported to be on the road per law enforcement from thunderstorms wind gust. Time based on radar estimate.
Report of a power pole knocked over and caught fire in Ashland due to strong thunderstorm winds. Time estimated by radar.

Law enforcement reported a tree fell on a power line in Cable and sparked a small fire which was quickly contained and was under control. No lane blockage with this report. Report estimated based on radar.
Powerlines reported down in Glidden due to strong thunderstorm winds. Time estimated by radar. Location approximate.
Powerlines reported down in Butternut due to strong thunderstorm wind gusts. Time estimated by radar. Location approximate.
Time estimated by radar. Reports of trees down of unknown size and quantity. Powerlines down due to strong thunderstorm winds.
Tree down in yard and over the owners car.
Tree on the roadway per law enforcement. Time estimated based on radar.
3 inch diameter branches broken.
Tree down on roadway per law enforcement. Time estimated based on radar.
Tree down on the road near Pike Lake. Time estimated based on radar per report from law enforcement.
8-inch tree limb down.
Photos of spikey hail. Longest axis along the spikes was 1.25 inches with a semi minor axis of about penny size.
Average hail size was between nickels and quarters with the largest being quarter-sized.
Time estimated by radar. Hail was found after the storm passed. Damage to vehicles was observed.
Hail lasted about 3 minutes.

Photos were received showing trees and large branches down in the area. One showed a tall white pine that was either uprooted or snapped near the base with others nearby that were snapped 5 to 7 feet above the base. Another showed a main branch of a tree approximately 3 feet in diameter that had been snapped near the base of the tree where it branched off. All trees appeared to be healthy in the photos. Time estimated from radar.
Multiple trees were reported down along Murphy Lake Road in the Central Lakes area. Trees appeared to be conifers that were snapped near the base. Power outages were reported in the area as well.
Hail ranging from pea size up to quarter size. Time estimated from radar.
Hailstone was measured next to a quarter. Spotter notes that the hail was slightly larger than the quarter.
Quarter size hail report from mPING.
Spotter reports dime to quarter sized hail.
Report via social media with photo. Time estimated from radar.
Report from social media of numerous 2 to 2.5 inch diameter hailstones with measuring tape photo.
Report via social media with video. Observer reports they have broken windows and a dented car. Time estimated from radar.
Photo was taken of quarter sized hail and was reported to be slightly larger than a quarter.
Photo via social media with measuring tape. Time estimated from radar.
Report via broadcast meteorologist viewer with photo. Time estimated from radar.
Report via social media with photo.
Spotter reports that damage occurred to siding and window trim. Roof damage was also reported. Photos included in report showing the size of the hail compared to a quarter.
Report with photo of ground completely covered with hail. Largest hail stone estimated up to golf ball size.

CORRECTS SIZE OF PREVIOUS GOLF BALL SIZE HAIL REPORT FROM 3 E SIREN FROM LAST WEDNESDAY EVENING. SPOTTER REPORTS DAMAGE OCCURRED TO SIDING AND WINDOW TRIM. ROOF DAMAGE WAS ALSO REPORTED. PHOTOS INCLUDED IN REPORT SHOWED THE HAIL AND QUARTER FOR COMPARISON.
Time estimated by radar.
Report via social media with photo.
Report from social media with photo.
Report from mPING: Quarter (1.00 in.).
Numerous tree branches were reported down with species being primarily white spruce and red pine. Branch size estimated to be around 2 inches in diameter. Time estimated from radar.

Spotter reports that several trees have been snapped. Some trees were snapped near the base while others were snapped near the top. The one tree that was snapped near the bottom has an estimated diameter of 10 inches. Time estimated from radar.
Time of report is estimated by a combination of radar and spotter report.
Report via social media with photo.
Hail damage includes severe damage to garden and trees. Possible damage to cars and roof.
Report via social media with photo.
Report of a tightly rotating mesocyclone and possible wind or tornado damage on Highway 169 approximately 2 miles east of Calumet. Location approximate. Time estimated by radar.
Quarter size hail. Location approximate. Time estimated by radar.
Several 8 to 10 inch diameter aspen trees down pointing from northwest and southeast. Time estimated from radar.

Delayed Report. High resolution satellite imagery depicts a swath of tree damage approximately a half mile long and 120 yards wide in a west northwest to east southeast direction just south of Rock Lake Road. Appears to be straight line wind damage based on storm evolution. Time estimated based on radar.
Spotter also mentioned that power was lost shortly after the quarter sized hail fell.
Small 5 inch diameter tree down blown down facing west. Time estimated by radar.
Spotter reports nickel to quarter sized hail.
Numerous hailstones with the largest stones up to quarter size.
Report from mPING: Quarter (1.00 in.).
Report sent in via social media.
Report from Tettegouche State Park with photos and video. Accumulating hail between 12:30 and 12:25pm with largest stone size up to 1 inch diameter. Most hailstones were around half inch diameter.
Report via social media with photo.
Reported golf ball to tennis ball sized hail along Highway 38 which caused dents in vehicle.
Report via social media with photo. Accumulating hail with primarily pea to nickel size, largest hailstones up to quarter size.
Report via social media with photo and videos. Time estimated from radar.
Report via social media with photo.
Mixed hail pea to nickel size with largest stones up to a quarter.
Corrects previous hail report from 6 ENE Lutsen. Report of dime sized hail with up to quarter sized hailstones.
Corrects previous hail report from 7 ENE Lutsen. Report of dime sized hail with up to quarter sized hailstones.
Corrects previous hail report from 7 ENE Lutsen. Report of dime sized hail with up to quarter sized hailstones.
A report of a few fresh trees down was received via social media along the Cascade River upstream of Highway 61. Time estimated by radar.
CoCoRaHS station MN-SL-232.
Some trees were snapped halfway up near the railroad tracks at McQuade Road. Time estimated by radar.
Corrects previous hail report from 4 NE Rice Lake.
Mature maple tree of around a 10 inch diameter down on northeast corner of UMD. Time estimated by radar. This corrects an earlier report that relayed a 10 inch radius maple tree down.
Mature maple tree of around a 10 radius down on northeast corner of UMD. Time estimated by radar.
Delayed photo of 1.25 inch diameter hail.
Several medium trees snapped at base and fell over powerlines. Time estimated from radar.
Corrects previous tstm wnd dmg report from 1 SW French River. Several trees were bent or snapped halfway up near the railroad tracks at McQuade Road. Time estimated by radar.
Report from mPING: Ping Pong Ball (1.50 in.).
Also had trees down in the area.
Via Social Media.
Corrects previous hail report from 2 SW French River.
Trees over powerlines along County N near Drummond. Time estimated from radar.
Half to one inch hail.
Numerous small branches and a few wrist size or larger branches down. Time estimated from radar.
Trees across roadway.
Reports multiple trees down uprooted and snapped at base. Time estimated from radar.
Video of winds ripping roof partially off. Time estimated from radar.
Many trees down Brandt Rd making the road impassible. Time estimated from radar.
Several trees down over roadway. Time estimated from radar.
Heavy rain causing surface runoff and minor flooding.
Reports of multiple trees around or less than 1 foot in diameter down along Highway 77 between intersection with Cty. Hwy OO and Forest Road 204. Time estimated from radar.
8 inch diameter 40 foot tall spruce tree snapped at base. Time estimated from radar.
Trees down over Lakeshore Dr.
Aspen tree snapped halfway up. Time estimated from radar.
Corrects previous hail report from 3 W Hinckley.
10 inch diameter aspen tree snaped off 6ft off ground. Time estimated by radar.
Multiple trees and large branches down on County Road CC. Some trees down over powerlines causing fires. Time estimated from radar.
Trees down on powerlines between Casino and Lake Road. Time estimated from radar.
Mesonet station DW6222 La Pointe.
Fallen medium to large trees blocking an entrance to Black Lake Campground. Time estimated from radar.
Mix of hail from pea sized to one inch diameter lasted for several minutes.
Tree down on powerline causing fire. Time estimated via radar.
Medium to large poplar trees snapped about 10 feet from the ground. Observe reports gusting swirling trees and 1.4 inches of rain. Time estimated from radar.
Storms resulted in trees down and a barn window blown in. Time estimated from radar.
Trees down across roadway. Time estimated from radar.
Several areas in Shell Lake with trees down across the roadway. Time estimated from radar.

Heavy rain in excess of 4 inches lead to several inches of gravel and dirt washed over Birkie rollerski track, and washouts along the side of the path up to a foot deep in the dirt with smaller chunks of pavement removed as well. Photos via social media. Time estimated via radar precipitation estimates.
Several medium to large washouts running across Boedecker road.
Running water from July 27 evening led to gravel washed across pavement on Highway OO and ditch became eroded. Time estimated from observer estimates.
Estimated 4 inches of rainfall.
Reports up to a quarter mile of Highway 35 covered with 6 to 10 inches of water.

Corrects location and time of earlier report from 4 SSE Ogema received Saturday. Location provided by spotter. Time estimated from radar. A photo was received via a TV partner of a hailstone and a tape measure. The largest axis of the hail was 2 inches in diameter.
Corrects location of previous hail report from Ogema. A photo was received via a TV partner of a hailstone and a tape measure. The largest axis of the hail was 2 inches in diameter.
A photo was received via a TV partner of a hailstone and a tape measure. The largest axis of the hail was 2 inches in diameter.
Mostly dime size hail with a few hailstones around quarter size. Time approximate. Storm started around two thirty PM and was ending around time of phone call around three ten PM.
Pea to quarter size hail. Time estimated from radar.
Spotter reports that a tree 12 to 14 inches in diameter was blown over. This tree then fell on a couple of power poles and broke them. Time of wind damage estimated from radar.
Few Branches down tho not big and pea size hail reported.
One Oak Tree snapped that was at least 12 inches in diameter. A Pine tree was also severely pushed to one side with some trees also pushed that direction in the area.
6 to 8 inch diameter tree fell, crushing a canvas tent. Time estimated from radar.
4 to 6 inch tree branches are down. Power flickering on and off.
Power Pole Snapped from winds.
Mentioned a few quarter sized hail.
large tree limb blown down. Time estimated by radar.
Measured wind gust from nearshore McQuade buoy.
Measured marine thunderstorm gust of 39 knots at offshore McQuade Buoy.
Trees and fence panels blown down. Time estimated from radar.
Fence panels were broken and blown into a window due to thunderstorm winds. Location approximate. Report received via Facebook. Time estimated by radar.
Report of tree down on powerline. Time estimated from radar.
12-13 inch Tree Snapped at the Base.
Report from mPING: Trees uprooted or snapped; Roof blown off. Time estimated from radar.
Report via social media with photo. Multiple mature poplar trees snapped 10 to 20 feet off ground. Time estimated from radar.

Wind speed estimated to be 65 to 70 mph based on damage. Multiple pine trees uprooted. Approx 12 to 18 inch diameter pine broken about 25 feet above ground. Empty hay wagon overturned. Some structure damage. Time estimated from radar.
Delayed report. Via social media of a toppled bush and several tree branches down. Time estimated from radar.
Pictures of three large and healthy trees uprooted and possibly snapped. A trailer was blown over on its side. Several pictures shared via Facebook. Location approximate.
Numerous trees 8 to 12 inches in diameter down and powerline down. Time estimated from radar.
Tree fell over the road on County Road H north of Highway 70 near Hertel. Location approximate.
Report from mPING: Quarter (1.00 in.).
4 to 5 small to medium trees down on Greenwood Lane. Time estimated from radar.
Tree down partially across road. Time estimated from radar.
Large dead pine across roadway with bumper in middle of road from car having hit it. Time estimated from radar.
Tree down blocking three quarters of roadway on County Highway E. Time estimated from radar.
Report from mPING: Tennis Ball (2.50 in.) DELETE THIS: Maybe true, waiting on further reports.
Tree down across Highway K. Time estimated from radar.
photo with hail next to a quarter.
Tree down across Post Avenue. Time estimated from radar.

Corrects previous tstm wnd dmg report from 4 ENE Stone Lake. This corrects the time that was previously incorrect. A branch from a tree was knocked down due to thunderstorm winds. Time is estimated by radar and the estimated time of about 10 PM from the observer. Report received via Facebook.
A branch from a tree was knocked down due to thunderstorm winds. Time is estimated by radar and the estimated time of about 10 PM from the observer. Report received via Facebook.
Corrects time of previous tstm wnd dmg report from Esko. Report of debris over a ramp at Exit 242 along Interstate 35. Also a report of two large trees about 12 inches in diameter down in Esko.
Report of debris over a ramp at Exit 242 along Interstate 35. Also a report of two large trees about 12 inches in diameter down in Esko.
Several branches down. Small tree split in two. Spotter also reported some damage to docks and pontoons at Devils Lake.
Several maple, oak, and pine trees blown down. Also had 1 inch diameter hail. Time estimated from radar.
also had several trees downed including oak, pine, and maple. Time estimated from radar.
Photo sent in via social media of an 8 inch diameter tree that was snapped. Observer notes that the tree already had a scar on it. Time estimated from radar.

Numerous trees were down onto power lines just off County Road 7 near Old Mesaba and Ramshaw Roads and the railroad tracks. Tree limbs ranged in diameter from 4 to 12 inches and were broken off about 20 feet off the ground. Trees were a mix of popples, pines and maples.
Strong winds with storms knocked several trees down along Vermilion Trail.
Report via social media with photos. Several small to medium sized trees down and reported power lines down as well. Time estimated from report and radar.
Numerous trees uprooted or snapped off along Bass Lake Rd. Time estimated off radar.
Numerous 12+ inch diameter pine trees snapped or uprooted. Trees downed on an outbuilding. Tree diameter estimated by photos. Time estimated by radar.
Vinyl siding pealed off of a garage.
12-18 inch diameter pine trees snapped. Diameter estimated from photo. Time estimated by radar.
Photos were received showing large tree limbs down in Hoyt Lakes. One limb looked to be at least 2 feet in diameter.
Large evergreen tree uprooted and fell on a garage. Estimated diameter of the tree is 14 to 18 inches from the photo. Time estimated by radar.
Multiple 8-12 inch diameter pine trees uprooted. Diameter estimated by photos. Time estimated by radar.
Multiple 8-10 inch pine and aspen trees snapped or uprooted. Diameter estimated by photos. Time estimated by radar.
Photo was received showing hail with a quarter with the hail being about twice as large as the quarter in diameter.
Storm blew the top of a white pine off. Time estimated off radar.
Many large trees snapped off 10 feet above ground. Trees uprooted in areas with thin or wet soils. Tree limb through metal roof of cabin.
Several large trees snapped and 24 inch tree uprooted.
Numerous trees were reported down on Boga Lake in the BWCA. Time estimated from radar.
Trees down around a campsite. Picture of a large tree snapped. Report seen on Twitter. Time estimated by radar.
Several trees snapped off. Report and photos via social media. Time estimated off radar.
Photos showing four pine tree trunks snapped about halfway up. Trees estimated one foot in diameter.
Photo sent in via social media of egg sized hail measured next to a golf ball.
Corrects previous non-tstm wnd gst report from 13 NE Sand Bay. Mesonet station DISW3 Devils Island. Marine thunderstorm wind gust from peak gust measurement.
61 knot wind gust measured on the Dirk S. VanEnkevort with a sustained wind speed of 54 knots.
Report via social media with photo. Observer states they had hail of all sizes up to golf ball and egg size shown in photo.
Multiple large trees down and power out. Time estimated from radar.
2 to 5 inches of water flowing out of side streets and onto Kenwood Avenue, covering 1 to 2 lanes of traffic. Reports still raining hard at time of observation.

Photos and videos via social media of large stream of water 3 to 10 inches deep rapidly flowing down the hill, inundating apartment buildings around 7th Ave and 3rd street. Fire Department conducting rescues to evacuate lower level residents and pets. Time estimated from video post time.

3 to 6 inches of water running across road where Chester Creek passes under Arrowhead Road. Still raining hard at time of report. Reports that it has been pouring for several hours on Duluth Hillside.

Video from Northern News Now showing at least several inches of fast moving water flowing down the Duluth hillside with overwhelmed storm drains pushing water back up into the road. Rain still falling at time of video. Time estimated.

MNDOT cams show standing and flowing water in and around Interstate 35 tunnels. Police closing the interstate through the tunnels and redirecting traffic off the road. Southbound lane appears fully covered with at least 4 to 6 inches of water.
Photos were received of rapidly flowing water on Pitt Street in the Lakeside neighborhood.

Photos and videos were received showing a large washout on Pioneer Junction Road where it crosses Schmidt Creek. The volume of water was more than the culvert could handle and opened a sinkhole before completely scouring a channel through the road. Time estimated.
The culvert carrying Schmidt Creek under the NSSR tracks was washed out. A train had passed about an hour before the washout affected the tracks and reported the developing washout. Time estimated.
Tischer Creek was out of its banks and onto St. Marie Street just west of Woodland Ave. Time estimated.
Corrects previous flash flood report from 3 S Rice Lake. Tischer Creek was out of its banks and causing extensive flooding at the Ridgeview Country Club.
Tischer Creek was out of its banks and causing extensive flooding at the Ridgeview Country Club.
Corrects location of previous tstm wnd gst report from 18 SSE Lutsen. Algoma Niagara. Steady 40kts gusting 55kts.
Gust of 55 kts. Sustained winds around 40 kts.
Algoma Niagara. Steady 40kts gusting 55kts.
Clyde S. VanEnkevort.
Visibility dropped to one quarter of a mile in heavy snow for 3 minutes. Winds were sustained at 20 mph and winds gusting to 32 mph.

Webcam at the intersection of US 53 and US 63 shows no snow on roads, then a brief period of very low visibility followed by snow covered roads indicative of the passage of a snow squall. Time is approximate.
WIDOT WI054 weather station reported visibility down to 0.14 mi in below freezing temperatures. Location approximate.

CoCoRaHS observer reported a glaze of ice on wooden surfaces in addition to snow accumulation in their morning observation, but without any freezing rain. This suggests a period of flash freeze may have occurred as a snow squall passed through. Location approximate. Time estimated by radar.
